AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Hair Dryer Brush, 5 in 1 Hair Deyer Styler, Black + Uv & Rubber Oil, Electric 3 Modes Blow Dryer Styler, One Step Hair Dryer Hair Straightener Brush and Blow Dryer Brush with Alci Plug for Women, Wig
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text:
I had a similar product which was recalled (rightly so as it heated up tremendously), this does not do that.
First it’s really meant to style not dry your hair. I towel dry, use a conventional blow dryer for a couple of minutes till damp then switch to the hot air brush and the decrease in frizz is awesome.
Really easy to use, especially for the back (yeah). I love it! It literally take no longer than five minutes even if I use my heated brush all told I’m in the 10 minute range, quite remarkable for curly, dense frizzy hair. Highly recommended.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.user_history_review: This is more of a hot-air styling brush than a true “5-in-1” system. It dries and smooths my shoulder-length hair reasonably well on the low/medium setting, but don’t expect salon-level straightening or a ton of tension like a flat iron. It’s easy to use and the handle feels comfortable, but the barrel gets hot fast so keep it moving and don’t use near the scalp/edges (especially for wigs). I also wish the listing was clearer on the plug/voltage situation. For the price it’s fine for quick blowouts, just not a precision tool.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: 1875 Watt Negative Ionic Hair Dryer, ENISECA Precise Wind Heat/Speed Setting Tech Professional Salon Blow Dryer Fast Drying Low Noise with Powerful AC Motor and Diffuser Comb for Women Men, Grey
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
I have fine, dry, flat hair that gets frizzy at the ends. I blow dry with a large round brush and need to switch back and forth between warm and cool settings often to style my hair. I wrap my hair around the brush barrel, blow it with heat and then use a cool setting to lock in shape. I’m constantly switching between and heat and cool settings. But I haven’t found a practical way to do my routine with this Eniseca hair dryer. This hair dryer makes a good initial impression based just on looks in that it’s lightweight, nice looking in black with rose gold, compact, digital and comes nicely boxed with accessories. But I’ve tinkered around with it for days and can’t find a workable way to toggle back and forth between my desired warm and cool settings. I haven’t been able to properly style my hair once with this dryer.

To explain, the heat setting comes on at 50 C, but I need it at about 76 C (max is 99 C). I have to press the plus button 26 times to get my desired heat level. After drying a section, I need a cool burst of air so I switch to cool air, but it defaults to 99 wind power. That’s way too strong, so I have to press the minus button 20 times or so to get a reduced usable air flow. It takes more time to adjust to the cool air flow than to actually use it. The worst part is that I then need to switch to heat again for the next hair section, but I can’t find a way to toggle back to 76 C as the heat button seems to default back to 50 C. So then I have to press the plus button 26 times again to get the right heat setting and that tedious cycle just continues. This is not a workable product for me. If there actually is a way to toggle between desired temperature and air flow settings and not be resetting constantly, then it’s far too complicated for me to figure out. I don’t like it. 2 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.user_history_review:
I have fine, frizz-prone hair and I blow dry regularly, so I’m picky about what class of dryer I’m getting (actual AC motor/powerful airflow vs a lightweight travel dryer that just gets hot). This ENISECA dryer reads like a real full-size, plug-in dryer with strong airflow. It dries my hair noticeably faster than my older basic dryer, and I don’t have to keep going over the same sections as long to get them mostly dry.

The heat/speed controls are straightforward and I like having more than the usual “low/high” feel. I mostly use medium heat with a higher airflow so I’m not overcooking my hair/scalp. The negative ion claim is hard to prove, but in practice my hair does look a bit smoother and less fluffy/frizzy than with my cheaper dryer, especially when I’m using a round brush.

It is not a tiny dryer, and with the AC motor it has some weight to it (not unbearable, but I wouldn’t want this for a gym bag). Noise level seems more of a lower-pitch dryer sound than a shrill whine, which I appreciate. Mine came with attachments (diffuser/comb) and they fit securely enough that I’m not worried they’ll pop off mid-use.

No weird smells, sparking, or overheating so far, and the cord length has been fine for my bathroom setup. I do wish the listing/packaging did a better job spelling out care (like cleaning the intake filter/grill), because that’s the kind of thing that determines whether a dryer stays powerful over time. Overall, this feels like a solid, fast dryer for routine home use at a reasonable price.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: AVCOO 4 Channel TENS EMS Unit 24 Modes Muscle Stimulator for Pain Relief Therapy, Electronic Pulse Massager Machine with 12 Pads, Dust-Proof Bag, Fastening Cable Ties.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: I did consult with my doctor and made sure it was okay to use the TENS unit. Read all the directions well before using it. I use it on my shoulder area the most. Sometimes lower back. It has a lot of different setting as to the rotation of the pulses. It can be very mild up to way to intense. I seems to like around level 10 or 11 in massage mode. Lot of pain relief in a tiny package. The sticky pads can be used by the same person a number of times if returned to plastic sheets between uses.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.user_history_review:
This is a real 4 channel TENS/EMS unit, not a little 2 pad toy. Mine came with 12 pads, the wires, a dust proof bag and little cable ties which I actually used to keep cords from turning into a knot. The modes are a lot so I just picked 2 that felt like a steady TENS and one “tap” style and stayed low until I knew how my body handled it. Pads stuck well and didn’t bite my skin.

Relieved my shoulder and low back tightness same day (not magic, but noticeable). I wouldn’t use this over broken skin or if you have a pacemaker. For the price it feels like a good set.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Ionic Hair Dryer, VLISBO 1875W Hair Blow Dryer Intelligent Temperature Heat & Wind Speed Settings Technology, Negative Ion Hairdryer with Powerful AC Motor for Hair Care with Diffuser for Home, Travel
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: This is a very powerful dryer but didn't make my hair all frizzy like my older dryer does. Has 4 different attachments and comes with a roller brush that is like discount store quality. The dryer itself is very good quality. I love that it comes a drawstring bag to keep everything together  and I can hang it inside bathroom closet.  Very happy with the results it gives me very quickly.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.user_history_review: This is a real AC motor hair dryer, not a tiny travel one, and it has solid heat and wind settings that stay where you put them. I used it on medium heat and it dried my shoulder length hair quick without that burnt smell, and the ion feature seems to help with frizz. Diffuser works nice for waves. It is a little heavier than my old dryer so I don’t hold it forever. Cord feels sturdy. Good value for the price.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: LOBKIN Lint Remover Rechargeable, Electric Sweater Shaver, Fabric Shaver Defuzzer 3-Leaf Stainless Steel Blades Lint Shaver Defuzzer Pill Fuzz Remover for Clothes, Dryer, Couch, Blanket, Sweate
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text:
Works great so far! I put it on charge before use. Didnt take long, and light turns green when its charged.
Lay the clothes flat, turned it on and went over a couple shirts with it in a circular motion. You could hear it when it cuts off a lint ball! Its not loud, Its easy to use, and safe to have around kids because there are no sharp pieces exposed unless you open it to clean it out.
Cleaning is also easy- you just unscrew the top and guide the included brush along the sides of it and toss the lint. I actually was shocked at how much came off just a couple of shirts. I'm glad this works because alot of my clothes started to look worn and ugly with the pilling. I've tried an actual razor but that was a pain in the bum. So I'm happy with this product and I've had no issues so far!
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.user_history_review:
Here are some key points in my overall experience with this lint remover:
- This is a rechargeable electric fabric shaver (not a sticky roller), and it does a good job taking pills/fuzz off sweaters and a throw blanket in 1–2 passes without snagging the fabric
- Simple to use and the head size covers a decent area so it’s quick for couch cushions and knits
- Battery has been fine so far for a few sessions; I like not having to deal with AA batteries
- The lint/fuzz collector is easy to pop off and empty, but you do need to clean it out regularly so suction/cutting stays consistent
- Blades seem sharp and the guard keeps it feeling safe on clothing, but I still wouldn’t press hard on delicate/loose weaves
- Packaging was minimal (appreciated) and overall it feels like a good value if you’re trying to extend the life of clothes rather than replace them

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Padgene Mens 11 in 1 Hair Clipper Electric Rechargable Quiet Detail Trimmer Professional Cordless Haircut Oil Head Head Shaver Grooming Kit for Heads, Beards & all Body Grooming (Super Grooming Kit)
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
We came to terms very quickly that we were going to use ONE attachment on this device. That attachment is the head shaver. No, I didn't use it on my head, but I did get good use of it as a body trimmer. The blades dull out really quickly and there is no cleaning solution included with this (unless I was just missing it from my box). When it is fully charged, it works VERY well and has a nice close shave without any issue. A few uses in though showed a bit of dullness that didn't hurt the skin, but instead "skipped" hair that should have otherwise shaved off.

This could be a great set if there was more quality control and a much, much easier way to change the attachments. We tried the nose/ear hair trimmer one during our initial use and found that it worked great, but we ended up breaking the attachment when trying to remove it.

There are better products on the market for all of these needs, and I'd recommend you check those out first.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.user_history_review:
This is basically an all-in-one cordless clipper/trimmer kit, not a “head shaver” in the foil/rotary sense. You’re getting a clipper body, a detail trimmer nose/edge style head, and a pile of guards/heads, plus a little oil/brush. Mine arrived intact and charged.

For the main job (haircut + beard line cleanup), it’s been solid. It cuts cleanly with the standard clipper blade and doesn’t bog down on normal density hair, and the detail head is precise enough to do neckline/cheek lines without feeling like it’s tugging. Noise is on the quieter side compared to the cheap corded clippers I’ve had.

Battery has been good for a couple sessions without needing a top-off, but I wouldn’t plan on this for barbershop all-day use. Cleaning is the usual: pop the head, brush it out, and oil it or it starts sounding dry.

Value is decent if you’ll actually use more than one attachment. If you only need one trimmer, the “11-in-1” is mostly padding.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: Sharper Image Spahaven Foot Bath, Heated Spa with Massage Rollers & LCD Display, 108 Degree Heat with Insulated Walls & Pumice Stone, Soothe & Rejuvinate Tired Feet, Aromatherapy Holiday Gift
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: This Sharper Image Acupressure Foot Massage Ottoman is a luxurious relaxing experience for tired feet. Whether you are just looking to put your feet up or add some heat or have a massage, it is versatile and compact. The convertible feature is convenient and I appreciate the ability to adjust the settings such as compression level and speed. I've used some other foot massages that do not have the top piece to keep your feet snug and it prevents it from allowing a deep massage. Luckily, this device helps to hold your feet in place and allow for a more powerful massage. Overall, it is a great device and I highly recommend it for anyone with tired feet after a long day or also it would make a wonderful gift.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.user_history_review:
This is a true electric foot bath/spa, not just a soak tub. Mine arrived with the pumice stone and the massage rollers in place, and the LCD/controls are straightforward. The main reason I bought it was heat retention, and it does hold a hot soak better than the basic plastic tubs I’ve used. It will maintain warmth up to the low 100s (108F claim seems like “max setting” territory, not what your feet will continuously feel), but it’s still a relax/soothe tool, not anything like a deep tissue massage device.

The rollers feel nice if you actively move your feet over them, but they don’t “massage” you passively. Cleaning is the main ownership cost: it’s easy enough to drain and wipe, but you do have to stay on top of rinsing around the roller area so it doesn’t get gross. Price is a little high, but it’s a solid upgrade if you actually use it.

AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.product_description: EXMAX H1B Massage Gun Deep Tissue Muscle Shoulder Percussion Massager Massagers for Neck and Back Massager for Back Pain Hand Held Professional Massager for Athletes Percussive Chiropractor Massager
AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.review_text: This is the most simple massage gun that I have gotten so far, but it is good for the price point. It works just fine and I've had no problems with it. If you are looking for a cheaper version of the high dollar massage guns, this is great.
AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.user_history_review:
This is a real percussion massage gun (not a gentle “vibe” massager). It has enough punch to loosen up tight shoulders/upper back after workouts, but it’s also pretty easy to overdo if you hold it in one spot too long. I mostly use a mid setting for 1-2 minutes per area.

Battery has been fine so far and it feels reasonably sturdy for the price. A little loud, but not awful. Overall good value if you want deep tissue and can handle the intensity.

AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.product_description: AW(AidallsWellup) Head Shaver, AW Men’s 5-in-1 Electric Head Shavers for Bald Men, Head Shavers for Men, Anti-Pinch, Ergonomic Design, Cordless, and Rechargeable.
AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.review_text: This head shaver was good while it lasted. Unfortunately, it didn't last very long, and broke after about a month of use. Some of the attachments were no good from the get-go, and were dull right out of the box. The head shaver itself wasn't dull, but again, sadly it fell apart pretty quickly.
AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.user_history_review:
This works like a basic rotary head shaver, not a magical “5-in-1” situation. It does get my head smooth pretty fast and I didn’t get the pinchy/bite feeling I’ve had with cheaper ones. Battery has been fine for a few shaves so far.

Cleaning is the annoying part. The head pops off, but hair collects inside and you’ll want to rinse/brush it out right away or it gets gross. For the price it’s decent, just don’t expect the attachments to be life-changing.

AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.product_description: Aiwejay S1 Mini Sonic Electric Toothbrush,Type- C Charging Port, IPX7 Waterproof，Pearl White
AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.review_text: I gave this toothbrush a number of tries before deciding I just don't like it. The vibrations feel intense, so much that they tickle my sensitive mouth and I have to keep my tongue out of the way. But then when I'm done, it doesn't feel like it cleaned all that well, which is confusing. I get a deeper clean just using my manual toothbrush. Also, the brush part is annoyingly small. It looks and feels sleek, and I like the wall mount it comes with, but it's just not for me.
AESI2BA4YODTHOSRFLJCSTAM6XDQ.user_history_review:
This is a true mini sonic toothbrush, not a full-size powerhouse, so set expectations. It’s been great for travel and keeping in my work bag. The Type-C charging is the main reason I got it, and it charges easily with the same cable I use for everything else.

Cleaning-wise it does a decent job for quick sessions, but it doesn’t feel as strong as my regular sonic brush at home. It’s also fairly gentle, which my gums appreciate. So far the battery has lasted me multiple days between charges. The waterproofing seems legit (rinses fine), but I wouldn’t leave it soaking. For the price it’s a solid little brush.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: AVCOO 4 Channel TENS EMS Unit 24 Modes Muscle Stimulator for Pain Relief Therapy, Electronic Pulse Massager Machine with 12 Pads, Dust-Proof Bag, Fastening Cable Ties.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: First off, I have to say that I approach this type of device with skepticism. I didn't have any experience with TENS units before, but wanted to give this a try for some shoulder pain that I experience. The pads that come with the unit are very sticky, and easy to apply. I also did like the size of the unit, as it's extremely small and portable, and with a little practice, not too difficult to figure out. I must admit, my skepticism has been tempered a little bit, because I would find that after a session, my pain would be reduced. That relief lasted a short while however, as it tended to reactivate not that long after. However, I was expecting no results at all. From that perspective, I can say that there is something to this technology. I wouldn't describe it as a cure, or absolute relief, but it certainly can provide a measure of relief that for some people may qualify as invaluable. I think that the size of the unit is ideal, and makes it extremely portable. I will continue to use it in the hopes that perhaps with prolonged use, I can achieve better results. My initial experimentations with it have been more positive than I expected. From that perspective, I'll say that I was pleasantly surprised.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.user_history_review:
This is basically a 4-channel TENS/EMS unit with a bunch of pre-set modes, and what showed up matched the listing for me: main controller, 12 adhesive pads, leads, a little dust bag, and cable ties. The fact that it’s 4 channel matters, because you can actually run two different areas at once (or mirror left/right) without constantly unplugging things.

I used it for lower back tightness and some post-workout soreness in my quads. The pulses feel more “therapy” than “massage,” so don’t buy this expecting it to replace a deep tissue session. But for pain gating / loosening things up, it did help me in the moment, and I liked being able to dial intensity per channel instead of everything moving together.

The learning curve is mostly pad placement and not cranking it up too fast. If you’ve never used TENS/EMS, start low and work up, because an uncomfortable zap is easy to create. Pads stuck well for a few sessions, but long term you’ll be replacing pads, so I’d factor that into cost. Overall it feels like a solid value if you want a multi-channel unit and will actually use it consistently.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: OTC Hearing Aids, Volume and Modes Adjustable, USA Made Digital Core, Rechargeable and Active Noise Cancelling, Pocket Size Hearing Amplifier for Seniors
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: These hearing aids arrived well packaged with an egg-shaped container and charging bay, two manuals, a micro-USB cable and wall plug as well as replacement earpieces and cleaning brushes. The hearing aids took less than one hour to fully charge which is indicated by the orange and green LED lights on the charging/storage container. The hearing aids are small enough to keep their use unnoticeable while still being large enough to handle easily. Being rechargeable, you don't have to mess with small replacement batteries that are difficult to install. The hearing aids work well and are easy to operate which is assisted by the quick instruction booklet provided. These are a little pricy but compared to others that I've tried I would say that these are worth the extra cost. I would recommend these hearing aids.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.user_history_review: Bought this for an older family member and the main thing to understand is this is really a hearing amplifier in a small case, not a “set it and forget it” medical-style hearing aid. It did arrive ready to use with a rechargeable setup and the controls for volume/modes were straightforward enough, but you do have to spend a little time dialing it in or the sound can get a bit sharp. The noise cancelling helps in a quiet room, but in busier environments it still boosts what you don’t want along with what you do. Comfort was decent for short periods, but they didn’t want to wear it all day. Battery life has been fine for daily use so far. For the price it’s OK as a starter/backup option, but I wouldn’t rely on it as a full replacement for properly fitted hearing aids.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: MHSY Electric Razor for Women, Shaver for Women Pubic Hair with 3 in 1 Wet & Dry Painless Rechargeable Hair Remover for Legs Underarms and Bikini Trimmer Line
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This rechargeable electric razor does look and feel cheap as others have mentioned. I do like the shape of it as it is easy to hold. I wish there was some texture on the body to provide a better grip. I used this to shave the bikini area and I thought it did a great job. The size of the shaver head was perfect for me for that area (much easier to maneuver compared to my old battery standard sized shaver head which I'd been using for years). I didn't experience any snagging of the hair as others have mentioned. The hair was pretty long but I just went over it with a light hand until it got short and then I got a close shave. I like that one side of the blade is curved for the under arms and bikini area (although I did those areas just fine with the straight side too). I think the biggest thing that I don't like is the way the blade/ foil is removed in order to clean. Mine was a mess inside because there was some kind of lubricant/vaseline type substance in there and the hairs were all stuck. I had to wipe all that gunk out. It wasn't the easiest the clean compared to my old shaver. I feel like this new one is not really meant to be cleaned or it would've had a better design to open up the shaver head. Another issue is that this is not to be used in or around water which is kind of inconvenient. I think the only thing I would put under water is the blade/foil once it's removed from the body. I wouldn't trust rinsing it under water while attached to the body like they show in the product video. On my unit when the blade/ foil is removed, I see a little piece of sponge/ foam in the center and underneath that it looks like it's opened into the handle. Definitely don't want to get water in there. I wish there was an included protective cover like my other shaver. I like that it is rechargeable by using the included micro USB cord. The handle glows red when it's charging and turns off when it's done. There is more to be desired but it gets the job done so it's not a total waste. I also don't know why the box says 'epilator' because it is not.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.user_history_review:
This is basically a small electric foil shaver with a few snap-on heads, not an “everything razor” like using a regular blade. Mine came charged enough to test right away and it charges via USB (no wall block). The heads click on/off easily and the unit is lightweight so it’s not intimidating to use in awkward areas.

On legs and underarms it does a decent job for quick maintenance, but it’s not as close as a fresh manual shave. For the bikini line/pubic area it’s more about trimming down safely than getting perfectly smooth. If you go in expecting “painless” and you use it on short hair with light pressure, it’s comfortable and I didn’t get nicks. If you try to rush it like a razor and press hard, you can feel it tug a little, especially on longer/coarser hair, so I’d recommend doing a first pass to shorten length and then a second pass if you want it closer.

Wet/dry is nice but I wouldn’t call it fully “rinse and done” because hair can collect around the head. I can tap most of it out and rinse the head, but you still have to check the edges/inside so it doesn’t build up. No irritation for me so far, but I also don’t use this to chase a completely bare shave.

Overall I like it as a gentle maintenance trimmer/shaver for sensitive areas and travel, just don’t buy it expecting it to replace a real razor for ultra-close results.

AHHQMVOQSY4WOIU5AWA5AEAQPJ6Q.product_description: Electric Shaver for Men, Keenhot Travel Men's Razor with PopUp Trimmer, Mini Shaver for Car Wet & Dry 2 in 1
AHHQMVOQSY4WOIU5AWA5AEAQPJ6Q.review_text:
I ordered this shaver for my husband to try, and we’ve had nothing but problems with it. It wasn’t charged at all upon receiving it, it was completely dead. We put it on the charger for several hours as the manual recommended and it worked.

While charging there is a red light next to where the cable plugs in, and the light stays lit all the time even after the shaver is charged. So you never knew if the battery if fully charged and whether or not it will overheat.

The shaver manual is not much help and in fact makes this product more confusing. The printed manual and writing on the shaver box have conflicting charging instructions. The manual says to charge up to 8 hours and advises no longer. The box says 1 hour for 60 minutes of shaving. This is kind of confusing because my husband was never able to get 60 minutes of use after charging. He was only able to get 2 shaves each at about 7 minutes before the battery went dead and had to be recharged.

So…. my husband will not be using this shaver since the battery keeps going dead. And the rotary blades are weakening from pressing the shaver against his face. This shaver is a no-go.
AHHQMVOQSY4WOIU5AWA5AEAQPJ6Q.user_history_review:
I bought this as a true travel/“keep in the car” shaver, and that’s really what it is. It’s a small foil-style unit with a pop-up trimmer, not something I’d rely on as my main daily shave at home. For quick clean-up (neck/cheeks) or a decent shave when you’re away, it does the job, but don’t expect barbershop-close results if you have heavier/stiffer beard growth.

It’s simple to use and the size is nice for a glove box or dopp kit. The pop-up trimmer is handy for sideburns and edging, although it’s small so it takes a little patience. I tried it both dry and with a little water and it felt more comfortable wet, with less tugging.

Cleaning is basically a quick brush-out and rinse (since it’s wet/dry), but you do need to keep up with it because it’s a small head and it loads up faster than a full-size shaver. Battery life seems fine for travel use so far, but I haven’t owned it long enough to say how it will hold up over months. For the price, it’s a reasonable little backup shaver.

AHHQMVOQSY4WOIU5AWA5AEAQPJ6Q.product_description: 9-in-1 Head Shavers for Bald Men w/ 7 Floating Heads, LED Display, USB Rechargeable, Mens Grooming Kit w/ Rotary Shaver, Nose Trimmer, Hair Clipper, Brush. Cordless Electric Razor Wet and Dry, Gold
AHHQMVOQSY4WOIU5AWA5AEAQPJ6Q.review_text: The appearance of this shaver is almost elegant.  My husband does not have a bald head so testing was only done on daily beard growth. All 7 blades revolving at once would lead one to hope shaving is quick. But the blades did not give him a close shave and took at least 8 minutes or more to finish.  He had to move the shaver slowly back and forward too many times.  He has another shaver but he was hoping that this 9-in-1 would be his favorite. The nose trimmer attachment and the beard trimmers work okay.  The battery life of this shaver having 90 minutes of shaver time is better than any other shaver he has used, but his Norelco shaver gives a super close shave in about 5 minutes.  I am giving this item 3 stars but not recommending it, although it may do a better job on shaving a bald head.
AHHQMVOQSY4WOIU5AWA5AEAQPJ6Q.user_history_review:
I bought this mainly to keep my husband’s head shave quick between barber visits. What showed up is basically a rotary head shaver with several clip-on attachments (nose trimmer, clipper, brush, etc.), so think of it as a kit and not 9 separate “tools.” The 7 floating heads do a pretty good job on a close buzzed scalp, and it’s comfortable as long as you don’t press hard. It will not replace a blade shave, but it gets him to an even, clean look fast.

The LED display is helpful and it charges by USB which is convenient. Battery life seems fine so far but we’ve only used it a handful of times. Cleaning is the main upkeep here: you really do need to pop the head open and rinse it out after use or it will hold hair.

Overall it works as advertised for quick head maintenance and is a decent value for the price, just keep expectations in the “rotary shaver” lane.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Esmartlife Foldable Squat Free Sitz Bath,Sitz Bath for Toilet Hemorrhoids Postpartum Care to Treat Postpartum Wounds, Hemorrhoids, Perineal Care, Episiotomy Recovery,Reusable Anal and Vaginal Care
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: The seat of the tub is amply sized. I have a big rear end and I fit on this just fine. I think the water stream is positioned more to the front, so you just need to position your body to get it into the right place where you need the stream to flow. I think the water stream pumps up high enough with the hand pump used in a free hand. It fits on my elongated toilet eat, and I assume on a standard as well. It will also sit directly on the toilet bowl. I like that it can fold up and become more compact. The ad/order page actually shows 3 different styles of this sitz tub (you can see 3 different hole patterns in the back of the seat). I received the one with 5 circle holes... I am sure they all function the same, but I don't know why the would depict them differently in this one order page. I removed a star based on this product depiction "sloppiness."  I do not understand what they mean by "no need to squat down."
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.user_history_review:
This is basically a foldable sitz bath basin that sits in/over the toilet, not a “mop and go” miracle. It arrived clean and intact, and it does fold down which is nice if you don’t have a lot of bathroom storage.

It does what it’s supposed to do for warm water soaks for hemorrhoids/perineal irritation, but you still have to set up your own routine (warm water, towel, timing). For me the big question was whether it feels stable on the toilet and doesn’t collapse when you shift your weight, and so far it’s been stable enough, but I wouldn’t treat it like something you can plop down carelessly.

Cleaning is straightforward because it’s just a basin, but you do need to wash and dry it well each time since this is obviously a body-contact item. No weird smell from the material. Price feels fair if it holds up over time. If you need something portable and reusable, this fits the job.
